pity and to love 'Twere surely found in thee

Unlike to those that cover cruel wiles
thy smiles , Unlike to those that cover cruel wiles , Plead

thy smiles , And early vice thy easy heart beguiles

saddened shade , A mournful gleam illumines the cold hill

thy temper to some direful deed

of kindness shed on your cold straw

your dark bed , children of woe

Lost mother , early doomed to guilt and shame , Whose friends of youth now sigh not over thy name
The smiles of peace and virtue they shall prove , Blessing
Approving spirits of the just behold
new virtues here , as on the wing Of morn , from Sorrow's dreary shades shall spring ; Young Modesty , with fair untainted bloom ; And Industry
These village virtues on the plain shall throng , And Albion's hills
